title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, golden brown with dimples, herbs, and glistening olive oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a slice of homemade Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, a taste of authentic Italian baking.

description: Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil is a true taste of Ligurian tradition, a rustic flatbread known for its irresistible chewy texture and crisp exterior. Bathed in fragrant olive oil and studded with fresh rosemary, it is the perfect accompaniment to any meal or a delightful snack on its own. This recipe brings the authentic flavors of Genoa right to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. Stir in the fine sea salt until dissolved.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Gradually add the flour to the yeast mixture, mixing with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Add 2 tablespoons (30ml) of the olive oil and mix until incorporated. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. The dough will be slightly sticky.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking
instructions.text: Pour 2 tablespoons (30ml) of olive oil into a 9x13 inch baking pan and spread it evenly. Gently transfer the risen dough to the pan, turning it once to coat with oil. Gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. If it resists, let it rest for 10 minutes, then continue stretching.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Dimpling
instructions.text: Cover the pan and let the dough rise for another 30-45 minutes. Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Once risen, use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the focaccia. Drizzle generously with the remaining 2 tablespoons (20ml) of olive oil, sprinkle with fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned and crisp. If the top browns too quickly, you can loosely tent it with foil.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, carefully remove the focaccia from the pan and transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy. Slice and serve warm, perhaps with a little extra drizzle of good quality olive oil.

tips: For authentic Ligurian focaccia, use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil. It makes a significant difference in flavor and aroma.
Do not over-knead the dough after the first rise when stretching it into the pan; be gentle to maintain its airy structure.
Store leftover focaccia at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days. Reheat gently in the oven for best results.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast of Italy, where the irresistible aroma of freshly baked focaccia fills the air. Among the many variations of this beloved Italian flatbread, Focaccia Genovese stands out as a true masterpiece, particularly when infused with the fragrant notes of rosemary and the rich essence of olive oil. This isn't just bread; it's a testament to simple, high-quality ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. If you’ve ever dreamed of recreating that authentic Italian bakery experience in your own home, mastering the art of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is your delicious starting point.

## What is Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil?

Focaccia Genovese is the original focaccia, hailing from Genoa, the capital of Italy's Liguria region. Unlike its thicker, often more bread-like counterparts found elsewhere, the true Genovese style is characterized by its incredibly thin, crisp crust, an impossibly airy and soft interior, and its signature dimpled surface. These dimples aren't just for show; they serve as tiny reservoirs for the generous amounts of extra virgin olive oil that are crucial to its texture and flavor.

The addition of "Rosemary Olive Oil" in its name highlights two non-negotiable elements that elevate this focaccia from good to utterly sublime. High-quality extra virgin olive oil is not merely an ingredient; it's a foundational component, contributing to the dough's elasticity, the crust's crispness, and the overall rich, fruity flavor. Fresh rosemary, on the other hand, provides an aromatic counterpoint, infusing the bread with its distinctive piney, slightly peppery notes that perfectly complement the olive oil. The combination results in a profoundly satisfying and aromatic experience that defines authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary.

Historically, focaccia was a humble baker's treat, often baked in the residual heat of wood-fired ovens before the main bread loaves. It was a simple, quick bread to be enjoyed plain or with a light topping. Today, it remains a staple, cherished for its versatility and its ability to transport you straight to the heart of Italian culinary tradition with every bite.

## Ingredients for Focaccia Genovese

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese requires a careful selection of ingredients. While simple, their quality makes all the difference.

*   **Strong Bread Flour:** Approximately 500g (about 4 cups). High-protein flour is essential for developing strong gluten, which gives focaccia its characteristic chew and allows it to hold those beautiful air pockets.
*   **Lukewarm Water:** About 350-400ml (1.5 to 1.75 cups). The exact amount can vary based on your flour and humidity. It should be warm enough to activate the yeast, but not hot.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (2.25 teaspoons or one sachet). This leavening agent is what makes the focaccia light and airy.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g (1.5 teaspoons) for the dough, plus extra flaky sea salt for sprinkling on top. Salt is crucial for flavor and also controls yeast activity.
*   **Sugar (Optional):** A pinch (about 1/2 teaspoon) can be added to the warm water to help activate the yeast, though it’s not strictly necessary if your yeast is fresh.
*   **High-Quality Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 60-80ml (1/4 to 1/3 cup) for the dough, plus a generous amount for the pan, dimpling, and drizzling. This is the soul of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary; do not skimp on quality here. A good EVOO will lend a beautiful aroma and flavor.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, needles stripped and roughly chopped. Fresh rosemary is paramount for that authentic, fragrant Focaccia Genovese Rosemary flavor.

## Step-by-Step Focaccia Genovese Instructions

Creating this Italian marvel is more straightforward than you might think. Patience is key!

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the lukewarm water with the optional pinch of sugar and the active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and 10g of fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in about 30ml (2 tablespoons) of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. It will be quite wet and sticky – this is normal for focaccia.
3.  **Knead (or Stretch and Fold):** Lightly flour your work surface or use a bowl. Knead the dough for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smoother and more elastic. Alternatively, for a no-knead approach, perform a series of "stretch and folds" in the bowl. Every 20-30 minutes for about 2 hours, gently stretch one side of the dough up and fold it over itself, rotating the bowl and repeating 3-4 times. This develops gluten without intensive kneading.
4.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare Pan and Second Rise:** Generously oil a 9x13 inch (or similar size) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil. Gently transfer the dough to the pan, trying not to deflate it too much. Gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan, working from the center outwards. If it resists, let it rest for 10 minutes, then continue.
6.  **Dimple and Top:** Using oiled fingertips, create deep dimples across the entire surface of the dough. Pour a generous drizzle of olive oil over the top, ensuring it pools slightly in the dimples. Sprinkle the chopped fresh rosemary evenly over the dough. For an authentic touch, you can also sprinkle a pinch of flaky sea salt. Cover the pan loosely and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes in a warm spot, or until visibly puffy.
7.  **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F). Just before baking, you can optionally drizzle a "brine" (a mix of 2 tablespoons water, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and a pinch of salt) over the focaccia – this is a Genoese secret for extra flavor and crust. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
8.  **Cool and Serve:** Carefully remove the focaccia from the pan and transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a little more fresh olive oil if desired. Slice and serve warm.

## Pro Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that bakery-quality Focaccia Genovese is all about attention to a few key details:

*   **Embrace the Stickiness:** Focaccia dough is high hydration, meaning it's very wet and sticky. Resist the urge to add too much extra flour during kneading or handling, as this will result in a dry, dense focaccia. Oiled hands are your best friend.
*   **High-Quality Olive Oil is Non-Negotiable:** We cannot stress this enough. The flavor of your extra virgin olive oil will shine through. Use the best you can afford, both in the dough and for drizzling.
*   **Don't Skimp on Dimples:** Make those dimples deep and plentiful! They are crucial for catching the olive oil and creating the characteristic texture.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** While dried herbs can work in a pinch, fresh rosemary provides an unparalleled aromatic burst that perfectly complements the rich olive oil in Focaccia Genovese Rosemary.
*   **Patience with Rising:** Don't rush the rising times. A slow, proper rise develops flavor and creates that coveted airy texture. The dough should look and feel light and puffy.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt:** A sprinkle of flaky sea salt (like Maldon) over the top before baking adds a delightful textural crunch and bursts of savory flavor that contrast beautifully with the rich bread.
*   **The Brine Secret:** Many traditional Genovese bakers drizzle a simple mixture of water, olive oil, and salt over the focaccia just before it goes into the oven. This creates an even crispier crust and adds an extra layer of savory depth.

## Serving Suggestions for Focaccia

Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways:

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve warm with a side of balsamic glaze, a good quality tapenade, or simply a bowl of your best olive oil for dipping.
*   **Alongside Meals:** It's the perfect accompaniment to hearty soups, fresh salads, or any Italian pasta dish.
*   **Sandwiches:** Its sturdy yet soft texture makes it ideal for sandwiches. Fill it with cured meats like prosciutto or mortadella, fresh mozzarella, roasted vegetables, or pesto.
*   **Breakfast or Brunch:** Enjoy a slice with your morning coffee, perhaps with a smear of ricotta cheese and a drizzle of honey.
*   **Plain and Simple:** Sometimes, the best way to enjoy this masterpiece is on its own, allowing the pure flavors of the olive oil and rosemary to shine through.

## Variations and Substitutions for Focaccia Genovese

While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations:

*   **Other Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you can substitute or add other fresh herbs like thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Toppings:** Before the second rise or just before baking, press in cherry tomatoes (halved), thinly sliced red onion, Kalamata olives, or a sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese. Roasted garlic cloves also make a wonderful addition.
*   **Flour Blends:** Experiment with substituting a small portion of the bread flour with semolina flour for a slightly coarser texture and nutty flavor, or whole wheat flour for added fiber and a heartier taste.
*   **Spices:** A pinch of red pepper flakes can add a subtle kick.
*   **Vegan Options:** Focaccia Genovese is naturally vegan, making it a fantastic option for those following plant-based diets.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While more challenging to achieve the exact texture, gluten-free focaccia recipes exist using specialized flour blends. Be aware the texture will differ from the traditional version.

Baking your own Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a truly rewarding experience. The process, from activating the yeast to pulling a golden-brown, aromatic slab from the oven, is a journey of simple ingredients transforming into something truly magical. So roll up your sleeves, embrace the sticky dough, and prepare to delight your senses with a taste of authentic Italian baking. Buon appetito!
